授業目的: このコースでは、学生たちは日本の伝統的な美の概念を、日本史の各時代と文化形式を通じてどのように表現され、実践されてきたかを考察し、習得します。主な美学・デザイン原理として「もののあはれ」「幽玄」「わびさび」「間」「真行草」に焦点を当て、学生たちは美の理念が文学、視覚芸術、建築、庭園デザイン、ファッション、精神・宗教的な儀礼と信仰、そして社会にどのように深く根ざしているかを理解します。また、学生たちは自身の美に対する感性と創造性を養います。
到達目標: 学習目標本授業を修了するまでに、学生は以下のことができるようになる。日本の主要な美学的概念について、その意味、歴史的起源、文化的発展、機能を理解する。これらの美学が、主要な芸術および文化的表現の中でどのように現れているかを特定し、分析する。日本の美や美学に関して、思慮深く批判的な問いを立てる。講義内容を踏まえ、分析的なエッセイを通して自身の理解を明確に表現する。日本の美をテーマとした共同の写真ギャラリー展示に参加し、美学的概念を創造的に応用する。
授業の概要・背景: This is a class in the discipline of Humanities and it educates the students in the subject of Traditional Japanese Beauty (through philosophical, historical, literary, artistic, aesthetic, psychological, and sociological lenses. Very few Japanese (or other) universities offer classes in Japanese Beauty or Japanese aesthetics, so this is a rare opportunity. The classes will give students a broad but thorough background in Japanese history up to the modern period, and its main cultural expressions. Students will learn how to evaluate these expressions in terms of their fulfilment of beauty standards and how to shape into an essay their acquired knowledge and their nuanced, informed opinions. This course helps students to acquire a foundational knowledge of Japanese culture, a specific mastery of its aesthetics, the ability to make sound aesthetic judgments themselves, and to understand the cultural specificity and function of those judgments. It will also allow students to hone their writing skills and their collaborative and curatorial skills (in creating an exhibition based on the course).
授業方法: This class uses lectures to deliver content, but there will also be discussions emerging from Q & A following the lecture or based on prompts given to accompany occasional activities such as analysing poetry or other visual or textual materials. We also have a regular short (5-10 minute) session of (casual, free) writing at the end of each class (which will be submitted to the instructor for feedback (notes on class may also be kept in the notebook, though students are free to type their notes). When photos are submitted (based on the readings about aesthetics, and one of the regular assignments) there will be viewings of some of these at the start of class, and the photographer can (voluntarily) give background information on their creative work. We will also have some Group Work focused on exhibition planning.

name: 【300】総合日本学習科目（日本の美）／JSC Traditional Japanese Beauty
担当者: ティンズリー　Ｅ．（TINSLEY ELIZABETH NOELLE）
成績評価備考:
備考　Note
Assessment
Presentations: 20%
In-class tests/essays (2): 30% each
Participation: (end-of-class notes (mandatory), discussion contributions, photo submissions (mandatory), exhibition collaboration (mandatory)): 20%
Notes on:
Attendance: Attendance is required. More than three unexcused absences lowers the final grade. Punctuality and active, respectful participation are expected.
Assignments: (1) Midterm (In-class test): Closed-book test on key Japanese aesthetic terms (definitions, examples, significance) identified in images. (2) Presentation: (Up to) 10 minutes plus Q&A. Topic related to course theme. Use at least two academic sources, provide context, include visuals, and pose a discussion question. (3) Final Exam (Essay): Open-book (which means use of your class notes taken throughout the course, and use of your end-of-class free writing, as well as any print-outs of class PowerPoints and assigned readings), handwritten, in class (Week 14). Choose from broad prompts; essays must show a clear thesis, structure, correct terminology, and engagement with readings and class materials.
Academic Integrity:
AI-generated writing for presentations and essays is not permitted. All work must be your own. Plagiarism results in failure and may be reported.
Evaluation Criteria for Essays:
A: Clear thesis, strong structure, insightful analysis of subject, effective evidence.
B: Solid thesis and organization, accurate but less original analysis.
C: Unclear thesis, mostly descriptive, limited evidence.
D: Poor understanding, weak structure.
F: Plagiarized or incomplete work.
